findings.title: ADR-0011 in AGENTS.md example commit message references non-existent ADR
findings.detail: AGENTS.md line 153 uses 'ADR-0011 for n8n vs Inngest' as a commit example. No ADR-0011 exists — only ADR-0001 through ADR-0010 are present. A worker reading this example may search for ADR-0011 or assume the n8n vs Inngest decision has already been recorded, silently skipping the required ADR creation step.
findings.fix_sketch: Change 'ADR-0011' to 'ADR-NNNN' to make clear the number is illustrative. The commit format is correct; only the number is misleading.

findings.title: NON_GOALS.md uses NG-N and NoGo-N naming inconsistently in same file
findings.detail: NON_GOALS.md contains two naming schemes: 'NG-7' and 'NG-8' (sprint-specific non-goals from the earlier section) and 'NoGo-1' through 'NoGo-10' (absolute non-goals). AGENTS.md line 140 references 'NG-7' which correctly points to the sprint-specific NG-7 section. However, a reader scanning the file for 'NoGo-7' will find a different entry than 'NG-7', creating potential confusion.
findings.fix_sketch: Add a brief heading or callout at the top of NON_GOALS.md explaining the two naming tiers: NG-N = sprint-specific non-goals (context-dependent); NoGo-N = absolute permanent non-goals. This prevents confusion without renaming.

findings.title: README.md says sprint 'completed 2026-05-21' but sprint ran through 2026-05-20
findings.detail: README.md line 7 states 'SPRINT-2026-05-A completed 2026-05-21 without full feature delivery'. The sprint end-date was 2026-05-20; 2026-05-21 was the reconciliation date. The wording 'completed' also implies success, whereas the sprint delivered nothing and was marked 'incomplete'. This could mislead workers about the sprint's terminal state.
findings.fix_sketch: Change to 'SPRINT-2026-05-A ended 2026-05-20 incomplete (reconciled 2026-05-21); next sprint targeting first operational build.'

findings.title: SPRINT-2026-05-B referenced in multiple files but never created (carried forward)
findings.detail: SPRINT-2026-05-B is referenced across SPRINT.md, ROADMAP.md, KPI.md, and .omc/STATUS.md as the next sprint to be created, but no SPRINT-2026-05-B.md file exists. As of 2026-05-31 this is 10 days past the 2026-05-21 reconciliation commitment. Workers looking for the active sprint file will find nothing.
findings.fix_sketch: Sebastian + Esme must create SPRINT-2026-05-B.md before the next work session. This requires human judgment about sprint scope and cannot be created by the nightly agent.

findings.title: ADR-0006 marked 'accepted' but its acceptance condition is still pending (carried forward)
findings.detail: ADR-0006 for Retell as default voice platform is marked 'status: accepted' but INTEGRATIONS.md notes the decision is 'pending Esme R1 confirmation'. No R1 research file exists and no deadline has been set. The accepted/pending contradiction means workers may treat Retell as confirmed while the validation is still open.
findings.fix_sketch: Either flip ADR-0006 to 'proposed' until R1 research is complete, or create a superseding ADR after confirmation.

findings.title: Retell R1 platform confirmation (Esme research) overdue with no deadline (carried forward)
findings.detail: The Retell R1 research task was planned for SPRINT-2026-05-A (ended 2026-05-20 incomplete). It is now 11+ days overdue with no new deadline set. INTEGRATIONS.md and ADR-0006 both depend on this confirmation.
findings.fix_sketch: Set an explicit deadline for Retell R1 research in SPRINT-2026-05-B when that sprint is created.

findings.title: RUNBOOK SLOs and alert thresholds are TBD with no process to define them before launch (carried forward)
findings.detail: RUNBOOK.md escalation triggers reference 'TBD per-product SLOs' and 'TBD per-alert thresholds'. KPI.md has no populated metrics. Without concrete thresholds, operators cannot determine Sev 1 vs Sev 2 during an incident, defeating the purpose of having a runbook.
findings.fix_sketch: Before M-016 (client launch), populate KPI.md with 3-5 baseline metrics and explicit alert thresholds. Link them in RUNBOOK.md trigger rules.

findings.title: Logging cross-cutting concern undefined — no schema, levels, or redaction spec (carried forward)
findings.detail: SECURITY.md mandates 'structured logs to stdout' and 'redaction' of PII, but no log schema, level definitions, or redaction rules are documented. Workers implementing logging in client repos will make inconsistent choices without a canonical spec.
findings.fix_sketch: Add a 'Logging spec' section to RUNBOOK.md or create a new LOGGING.md defining: log levels (debug/info/warn/error), structured JSON schema, PII field redaction rules, and retention targets.
